message:
Last week my kid's school called during the one moment my phone decided to die—battery at 1%, and of course I didn't have the charger in the car. Cue mild panic while I'm scrambling to find a pay phone (do those even exist anymore?) to call back.

It got me thinking: how many important numbers or details do we actually keep *outside* our phones? I realized I didn't even know my own backup contact's number without looking it up.

So I started keeping a simple written list of essentials—emergency contacts, important dates, insurance info—tucked in a drawer at home and a copy in my go-bag. Nothing fancy. Just the stuff that matters when technology takes a coffee break.

Maybe it's old-school, but there's something reassuring about having a backup plan that doesn't need charging.

Do you keep any important info written down the old-fashioned way, or are you all-in on digital?
